slice_title: R3 graph-retrieval arm + ELPS conformance gate

deliverables.RED-1_ELPS_conformance_gate: PASS — elps_golden_all_8_cases_ingest_no_orphaned_edges: all 8 cases (d1-d8) ingest with orphaned==0 and round-trip fidelity; golden.jsonl + golden_inputs.jsonl vendored from Memex; stub_harness.py maps source_doc_id to expected result
deliverables.RED-2_three_arm_RRF: PASS — fuse_three_arms() public function with RRF_WEIGHT_GRAPH=1.0; fuse_rrf() delegates to fuse_three_arms(v,t,vec![]); determinism test + empty-graph-arm byte-identity test + rrf_graph_arm_expands_ranking integration test all GREEN
deliverables.RED-3a_temporal_filter: PASS — graph_arm_drops_invalidated_edges: edges with t_invalid=2000-01-01T00:00:00Z excluded from BFS traversal; bob target node does NOT appear in results
deliverables.RED-3b_temporal_fallback: SCHEMA-GATE (IGNORED) — graph_arm_temporal_fallback_excluded_or_downweighted: #[ignore] because detecting temporal_fallback edges at BFS time requires canonical_edges.temporal_fallback BOOLEAN column (SCHEMA_VERSION 14→15 = HITL-required schema gate). Test documented as intended behavior
deliverables.RED-3c_byte_identity: PASS — graph_arm_disabled_is_byte_identical_to_baseline: use_graph_arm=false results match Engine::search() exactly

engine_changes: fuse_rrf() delegates to fuse_three_arms(v, t, vec![]) for backward compat
Engine::search_reranked() gains use_graph_arm: bool parameter (default false)

bfs_graph_arm_candidates() function: BFS over canonical_edges, depth<=3, cap 50, temporal filter (t_invalid IS NULL OR t_invalid > datetime('now')), synthesized-node penalty (kind='unknown' => score*=0.3)
sdk_changes: PyO3 binding (fathomdb-py): use_graph_arm: bool param on search() with default false
PyO3 binding: t_valid/t_invalid passed through translate_edge() — FIX: these were hardcoded None before
NAPI binding (fathomdb-napi): useGraphArm: Option<bool> param on search()
NAPI binding: tValid/tInvalid + t_valid/t_invalid passed through translate_edge() — same FIX
Python wrapper (engine.py): use_graph_arm: bool = False with isinstance(bool) validation (TypeError)
TypeScript wrapper (index.ts): useGraphArm?: boolean with typeof !== 'boolean' guard (TypeError), 'graph_arm' in SoftFallbackBranch type
binding.ts: useGraphArm?: boolean added to NativeEngine.search() interface
_fathomdb.pyi: use_graph_arm: bool = ... added to Engine.search() stub
test_changes: tests/elps_conformance_golden.rs: RED-1 conformance gate (8 golden cases)
tests/pr_g9_rrf_fusion.rs: RED-2 tests (fuse_three_arms determinism, byte-identity, expand-ranking integration)
tests/slice30_graph_arm.rs: RED-3a/3b/3c/4 tests (temporal filter, schema-gate ignore, byte-identity, CDF artifact pin)
doc_changes: dev/design/slice-30-design.md: overwritten with R3 graph-arm design memo (was 0.8.0 G2/G3)
docs/reference/python-api.md: search() signature updated, SoftFallbackBranch comment updated
docs/reference/typescript-api.md: search() signature updated, SoftFallbackBranch comment updated

blockers_encountered.description: temporal_fallback field detection at BFS time requires a new canonical_edges.temporal_fallback BOOLEAN column (SCHEMA_VERSION 14 → 15). This is a schema migration requiring HITL approval per the schema gate in §3.0.R.
blockers_encountered.disposition: Test marked #[ignore] with explanation. All other deliverables implemented. Deferred to SCHEMA_VERSION 15 (separate HITL gate).

gate_results.cargo_test_fathomdb_engine: PASS (all test suites OK, 1 test ignored per SCHEMA-GATE-1)

notes: t_invalid/t_valid were silently dropped by both PyO3 and NAPI translate_edge() functions (hardcoded None). This was discovered when the Python functional test test_graph_arm_drops_expired_edge failed because expired edges were not being persisted as expired. Fixed in commit 65d71b9.
The IR-C-recall-cdf.json path in slice30_graph_arm.rs had 5 levels of '../' but should have 4 (CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR is 4 levels below the worktree root). Fixed in the GREEN commit.
bfs_graph_arm_candidates() uses a separate DEFERRED transaction after the main search transaction commits, avoiding borrow conflicts with the main tx.

For the use_graph_arm=true path, the fusion uses fuse_three_arms(two_arm_fused, vec![], graph_candidates) — the already-fused two-arm result is passed as the 'vector' arm with an empty 'text' arm. This preserves all existing ranking semantics while the graph arm contributes new candidates.
